title: Plan money transfer from South Africa
shortDescription: International transfers from South Africa often need earlier planning; compare options and allow time.
whyItMatters: Many movers from South Africa need to move funds for deposit and first months; timing and limits vary.
whatThisInvolves: Compare transfer providers and limits
Allow time for any bank or regulatory steps
Keep a buffer in an accessible account

title: Keep South African bank access active during transition
shortDescription: Many movers prefer to keep SA account access until Dutch banking is in place.
whyItMatters: Having access to funds and statements can help with first months and any tax or admin follow-up.
whatThisInvolves: Notify your bank of your move if required
Ensure cards work abroad for the transition period
Plan when to close or change accounts if needed
whenToDoIt: Before departure and through first weeks in NL.

title: Review SA-issued civil record access and certification
shortDescription: South African civil records may need apostille or legalization; allow time for DHA and logistics.
whyItMatters: Dutch authorities often require certified or apostilled documents; country-specific timing can vary.
whatThisInvolves: Check what is needed for birth, marriage, or other certificates
Allow for DHA and apostille processing times
Order early and keep copies

title: Prepare for long-haul relocation buffer
shortDescription: Long-distance moves often benefit from extra buffer for admin, shipping, and first weeks.
whyItMatters: Flights, shipping, and document processing can take longer; a buffer reduces stress.
whatThisInvolves: Allow extra time in your timeline for delays
Plan budget for unexpected costs
Keep key documents and funds accessible

title: Review US banking and tax follow-up before moving
shortDescription: US citizens may have ongoing tax and reporting obligations; many keep US financial access stable during transition.
whyItMatters: Being aware of US tax and banking implications can help you plan accounts and timing.
whatThisInvolves: Check if you need to notify your bank or update address
Be aware of any tax or reporting implications of moving
Consider keeping a US address or mail service if required by institutions

title: Keep US financial access stable during transition
shortDescription: Ensure US cards and accounts remain usable abroad until you have Dutch banking in place.
whyItMatters: Avoid blocked cards or closed accounts when you need access most.
whatThisInvolves: Notify US bank of international move and travel dates
Confirm international use and any limits
Update address or use mail-forwarding if required

title: Review US-issued records and apostille if relevant
shortDescription: US-issued documents may need apostille for use in the Netherlands; check requirements early.
whyItMatters: Dutch authorities often require apostille or certified copies for foreign documents.
whatThisInvolves: Check what the IND or municipality requires
Obtain apostille from the relevant US state or authority
Allow time for processing
whenToDoIt: 2–3 months before move if documents are needed.

title: Review India-issued document readiness
shortDescription: India-issued documents often need attestation or legalization; start early.
whyItMatters: Document attestation and processing times in India can vary; starting early avoids delays.
whatThisInvolves: Identify which documents need attestation or apostille
Allow time for MEA and state-level processing
Keep certified copies and originals in your document pack

title: Plan international transfer and banking access (India)
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds from India and maintain access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Transfer limits and banking rules can affect when and how you move funds.
whatThisInvolves: Check RBI and bank rules for international transfers
Compare transfer providers and timing
Plan first-month access to funds in NL

title: Review UAE and home-country document readiness
shortDescription: UAE-issued documents often need MOFA attestation; documents from your passport country may follow a different legalisation path.
whyItMatters: MVV and municipality steps are sensitive to correct legalisation and translations; mixed passport + UAE residence cases need a clear document map.
whatThisInvolves: List documents by issuing country (UAE vs passport country)
Plan MOFA attestation or apostille steps as applicable
Book sworn translation if Dutch authorities require Dutch or English

title: Plan international transfer and banking access (UAE)
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds from the UAE and maintain account access during the transition.
whyItMatters: UAE bank rules, salary final payments, and exchange timing can affect your first months in the Netherlands.
whatThisInvolves: Confirm employer final settlement and transfer timing
Compare transfer providers and corridor pricing
Plan first-month liquidity in EUR for deposit and living costs

title: Review UK account and provider transition
shortDescription: Notify UK banks and key providers of your move; confirm international use and any address requirements.
whyItMatters: Keeping UK accounts usable during the move can help with transfers and first months.
whatThisInvolves: Notify bank of move and overseas use
Update address or use mail forwarding if needed
Check any tax or reporting implications of non-residence

title: Check UK driving licence awareness for later
shortDescription: If you plan to drive in the Netherlands, be aware that UK licence exchange rules apply; you can confirm details after arrival.
whyItMatters: Exchange or conversion may be required within a certain period; the Arrival Planner can cover first-week priorities first.
whatThisInvolves: Note that you may need to exchange or convert your UK licence
Check RDW or government guidance when you are closer to arrival
Focus first on registration, BSN, and banking
whenToDoIt: Before or after arrival; not urgent for pre-move checklist.

title: Review Canada banking and document access
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds and keep Canadian document access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Smooth transition of banking and documents helps first months in the Netherlands.
whatThisInvolves: Notify bank of move; confirm international use
Plan transfer of funds and timing
Keep access to key documents and statements

title: Review Australia banking and document access
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds and keep Australian document access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Long-distance moves benefit from clear banking and document planning.
whatThisInvolves: Notify bank of move; confirm international use
Plan transfer of funds and timing
Keep access to key documents and statements

title: Confirm cross-border document and financial access
shortDescription: As an EU/EEA mover, confirm your documents and bank cards will work for the transition.
whyItMatters: Lighter visa complexity still requires valid ID and access to funds.
whatThisInvolves: Ensure passport or ID is valid
Confirm bank cards work in the Netherlands
Have proof of address and employment ready for registration

title: Confirm arrival registration address
shortDescription: Even with lighter logistics, confirm where you will register after arrival.
whyItMatters: Registration is required for everyone; confirming your address in advance speeds up first steps.
whatThisInvolves: Confirm first address and that registration is allowed
Have rental contract or proof of address ready
Note municipality and how to book registration

title: Plan fund transfer and document access (Brazil)
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds from Brazil and keep document access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Transfer and document certification from Brazil can take time; planning early helps.
whatThisInvolves: Check central bank and bank rules for international transfers
Compare transfer providers
Allow time for any document certification or apostille

title: Review Mexico-issued document readiness
shortDescription: Mexican actas and official records often need Hague apostille; start early for MVV and municipality steps.
whyItMatters: IND and gemeente timelines slip when apostille or translation steps are queued behind consular appointments.
whatThisInvolves: List documents by issuing authority (state vs federal)
Book apostille steps for each record type
Plan sworn translation if Dutch or English is required

title: Plan international transfer and banking access (Mexico)
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds from Mexico and keep account access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Exchange controls, transfer limits, and employer final payments can affect your first months in the Netherlands.
whatThisInvolves: Compare transfer providers and corridor pricing
Confirm employer payroll timing if applicable
Keep first-month EUR liquidity for deposit and rent

title: Review Singapore-issued document readiness
shortDescription: Singapore public documents often need a Hague apostille through SAL for Dutch use; start early for MVV and municipality steps.
whyItMatters: IND and gemeente timelines slip when apostille or translation queues run behind permit decisions.
whatThisInvolves: List documents by issuing authority
Book SAL apostille steps where legalisation is required
Plan sworn translation if Dutch or English is required

title: Plan international transfer and banking access (Singapore)
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds from Singapore and keep account access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Employer payouts, CPF considerations where relevant, and first-month EUR needs can overlap during the move window.
whatThisInvolves: Compare transfer providers and corridor pricing
Confirm employer payroll timing if applicable
Keep first-month EUR liquidity for deposit and rent

title: Review Japan-issued document readiness
shortDescription: Japanese public documents often need a Hague apostille through MOFA for Dutch use; start early for MVV and municipality steps.
whyItMatters: IND and gemeente timelines slip when apostille or translation queues run behind permit decisions.
whatThisInvolves: List documents by issuing authority
Book MOFA apostille steps where legalisation is required
Plan sworn translation if Dutch or English is required

title: Plan international transfer and banking access (Japan)
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds from Japan and keep account access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Employer payouts, Japanese bank procedures, and first-month EUR needs can overlap during the move window.
whatThisInvolves: Compare transfer providers and corridor pricing
Confirm employer payroll timing if applicable
Keep first-month EUR liquidity for deposit and rent

title: Review South Korea–issued document readiness
shortDescription: South Korean public documents often need a Hague apostille and Korean originals usually need translation for Dutch use; start early for MVV and municipality steps.
whyItMatters: IND and gemeente timelines slip when apostille, translation, or embassy queues run behind permit decisions.
whatThisInvolves: List documents by issuing authority
Book apostille steps where legalisation is required
Plan sworn translation into Dutch, English, French, or German when required

title: Plan international transfer and banking access (South Korea)
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds from South Korea and keep account access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Employer payouts, Korean bank procedures, and first-month EUR needs can overlap during the move window.
whatThisInvolves: Compare transfer providers and corridor pricing
Confirm employer payroll timing if applicable
Keep first-month EUR liquidity for deposit and rent

title: Review Türkiye-issued document readiness
shortDescription: Netherlands Worldwide explains that some Turkish documents can be used in the Netherlands immediately, while others need apostille; Turkish originals generally need translation into Dutch, English, French, or German when required—start early for MVV and municipality steps.
whyItMatters: IND and gemeente timelines slip when apostille, translation, or appointment queues run behind permit decisions.
whatThisInvolves: List documents by issuing authority
Book apostille steps where legalisation is required
Plan sworn translation when Dutch authorities require an accepted language

title: Plan international transfer and banking access (Türkiye)
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds from Türkiye and keep account access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Employer payouts, Turkish bank procedures, and first-month EUR needs can overlap during the move window.
whatThisInvolves: Compare transfer providers and corridor pricing
Confirm employer payroll timing if applicable
Keep first-month EUR liquidity for deposit and rent

title: Review Argentina-issued document readiness
shortDescription: Netherlands Worldwide explains that documents from Argentina need a digital e-apostille from the Argentine Ministry of Foreign Affairs for use in the Netherlands; translations may be required—start early for MVV and gemeente steps.
whyItMatters: Embassy and IND timelines slip when e-apostille or translation steps run behind permit decisions.
whatThisInvolves: List documents by issuing authority
Complete MFA digital e-apostille where legalisation is required
Plan sworn translation when Dutch authorities require an accepted language

title: Plan international transfer and banking access (Argentina)
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds from Argentina and keep account access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Employer payouts, Argentine bank procedures, and first-month EUR needs can overlap during the move window.
whatThisInvolves: Compare transfer providers and corridor pricing
Confirm employer payroll timing if applicable
Keep first-month EUR liquidity for deposit and rent

title: Review Chile-issued document readiness
shortDescription: Netherlands Worldwide explains that documents from Chile must be legalised by the Chilean authorities with a Hague apostille before use in the Netherlands; translations may be required—start early for MVV and gemeente steps.
whyItMatters: Embassy and IND timelines slip when apostille or translation queues run behind permit decisions.
whatThisInvolves: List documents by issuing authority
Complete apostille steps where legalisation is required
Plan sworn translation when Dutch authorities require an accepted language

title: Plan international transfer and banking access (Chile)
shortDescription: Plan how you will move funds from Chile and keep account access during the transition.
whyItMatters: Employer payouts, Chilean bank procedures, and first-month EUR needs can overlap during the move window.
whatThisInvolves: Compare transfer providers and corridor pricing
Confirm employer payroll timing if applicable
Keep first-month EUR liquidity for deposit and rent

title: Coordinate employer documents and start date
shortDescription: Align employer-sponsored route, required documents, and start date.
whyItMatters: Sponsorship often depends on timely documents from both you and the employer.
whatThisInvolves: Confirm list of documents employer or IND needs
Agree on start date and permit timeline
Provide certified copies or originals as required
whenToDoIt: As soon as you have an offer.

title: Confirm sponsored route and required documents
shortDescription: Confirm exactly which documents the sponsor and IND need and in what form.
whyItMatters: Missing or incorrect documents can delay your permit and start date.
whatThisInvolves: Get checklist from employer or IND
Gather and certify documents as required
Submit in line with timeline
whenToDoIt: As soon as sponsorship is confirmed.

title: Plan salary access for first month
shortDescription: Confirm how you will receive first salary if Dutch account is not yet open.
whyItMatters: Some employers can pay into a non-Dutch account initially; confirm with HR.
whatThisInvolves: Ask employer if they can pay into foreign account for first month if needed
Know when they need BSN and Dutch account details
Plan buffer if there is a delay

title: Review remote or transfer admin implications
shortDescription: If you are already employed (e.g. remote or transfer), confirm what your employer needs for the move.
whyItMatters: Employers may need updated contract, address, or tax details for the Netherlands.
whatThisInvolves: Confirm with HR what changes with your move
Provide new address and BSN once you have them
Align start of Dutch contract or tax registration with arrival

title: Confirm employer records and arrival timeline
shortDescription: Confirm your employer has your arrival date and knows what documents you will provide when.
whyItMatters: Smooth onboarding depends on clear communication on both sides.
whatThisInvolves: Share arrival date and first-day plans
Confirm what they need from you (BSN, bank details) and by when
Note contact for admin questions

title: Strengthen funding buffer and housing flexibility
shortDescription: If you are still searching for work, plan a larger buffer and flexible housing options.
whyItMatters: Job search timing can be uncertain; a buffer and flexible housing reduce pressure.
whatThisInvolves: Estimate how long you can cover costs without local salary
Consider short-term or flexible rental at first
Keep documents and CV ready for applications

title: Prepare education and CV supporting records
shortDescription: Have diplomas, certificates, and CV ready for job applications and any recognition procedures.
whyItMatters: Employers and recognition bodies may ask for proof of qualifications.
whatThisInvolves: Update CV for Dutch or international applications
Gather certified copies of diplomas
Check if your sector requires recognition in the Netherlands

title: Gather child identity and civil records
shortDescription: Collect children's IDs, birth certificates, and any documents needed for school or family registration.
whyItMatters: Schools and municipality often require these for registration.
whatThisInvolves: Birth certificates for each child
Passports or ID documents
Any custody or guardianship documents if relevant

title: Prepare school or childcare background docs
shortDescription: Have previous school reports or childcare references ready if schools ask for them.
whyItMatters: Some schools or childcare providers ask for previous records.
whatThisInvolves: Request copies of last school reports or references
Translate if required
Keep in document pack

title: Confirm what family documents may be needed after arrival
shortDescription: Note which family documents you will need for municipality and school registration.
whyItMatters: Having them ready speeds up registration and school sign-up.
whatThisInvolves: List: birth certificates, marriage certificate, passports
Ensure they are in your hand luggage document pack
Check municipality and school websites for exact list

title: Prioritise temporary housing plan
shortDescription: If you do not have a place yet, focus on finding short-term or temporary accommodation first.
whyItMatters: You need an address to register; temporary housing can bridge the gap until you find something permanent.
whatThisInvolves: Research short-term rentals, serviced apartments, or house-sits
Check whether registration is allowed at short-term addresses
Book at least first weeks before you travel
whenToDoIt: As soon as possible; intensify 1–2 months before move.

title: Confirm how registration may work after arrival
shortDescription: Understand how you can register if you start with temporary or no fixed address.
whyItMatters: Municipalities have different rules for short-stay or temporary addresses; knowing in advance helps.
whatThisInvolves: Check your expected municipality's rules for registration
Understand if you can register at a short-stay address
Plan to move to a registerable address as soon as you can

title: Keep housing docs flexible and ready
shortDescription: Have proof of address or booking confirmation ready for whatever your first address is.
whyItMatters: Registration and some services need proof of where you are staying.
whatThisInvolves: Keep rental contract or booking confirmation in document pack
Ensure it states you can register if that is the case
Have landlord or host contact details
whenToDoIt: As soon as you have a confirmed place.

title: Confirm temporary housing booking and duration
shortDescription: Confirm your temporary housing booking and how long you can stay and register there.
whyItMatters: You need to know when you must find a next address and whether you can register at the temporary one.
whatThisInvolves: Confirm check-in and check-out dates
Clarify with landlord if registration is allowed
Plan next step (extend or find permanent place)

title: Check what proof of address you will have
shortDescription: Ensure you will have acceptable proof of address for the municipality.
whyItMatters: Registration appointments often require a rental contract or written confirmation from the landlord.
whatThisInvolves: Get written confirmation or contract from landlord
Ensure it includes your name, address, and that you can register
Bring to registration appointment

title: Decide what goes in shipment vs cabin luggage
shortDescription: Essential documents and first-week essentials must stay with you — never in shipment.
whyItMatters: Shipments can be delayed or held; you need documents and basics from day one.
whatThisInvolves: List: documents, medication, first-week clothes, chargers, keys
Pack these in hand or checked luggage
Do not put passport, visa, or proof of address in shipment

title: Keep essential documents out of shipment
shortDescription: Never pack passport, visa, proof of address, or employment docs in your shipment.
whyItMatters: You need these at the border and for first appointments; shipment can be delayed.
whatThisInvolves: Double-check document pack is in hand luggage
Do not put originals or only copies in boxes
Keep digital backups accessible

title: Track relocation or shipping provider
shortDescription: Keep shipment reference and carrier contact to hand for delivery and any customs questions.
whyItMatters: You may need to coordinate delivery or provide documents.
whatThisInvolves: Save tracking number and carrier contact
Know estimated delivery window
Have invoice or inventory if customs ask

title: Compare money transfer options early
shortDescription: If you will move larger savings or funds, compare providers and timing well in advance.
whyItMatters: Limits, fees, and timing vary; planning early can save cost and stress.
whatThisInvolves: Compare fees and exchange rates
Check limits per transaction and per period
Plan how much to move when

title: Keep emergency cash buffer accessible
shortDescription: Have an emergency buffer in an account or card you can access immediately after arrival.
whyItMatters: Unexpected costs or delays can arise; accessible buffer reduces stress.
whatThisInvolves: Ensure at least one card or account works abroad
Keep a small amount of local currency for first days if useful
Do not put all funds in a single channel that might be delayed

title: Prioritise core document gathering
shortDescription: Focus on passport, ID, and any visa or residence documents first.
whyItMatters: Many other steps depend on having these in order.
whatThisInvolves: List what you need for your route (passport, visa, employment letter, etc.)
Gather and certify as required
Use the Document Readiness Checker for a full list tailored to your situation
whenToDoIt: As soon as you decide to move.

title: Use the Document Readiness Checker
shortDescription: Get a full document checklist tailored to your situation with the Document Readiness tool.
whyItMatters: The Document Readiness Checker gives detailed document lists and reduces the risk of missing something.
whatThisInvolves: Open the Document Readiness Checker tool
Enter your situation (origin, route, household)
Work through the list and tick off what you have
whenToDoIt: As soon as you start preparing.
